_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of organizational cybersecurity goals and objectives
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI governance structures, including model risk management, AI lifecycle oversight, accountability assignment, and the organizational conditions that enable responsible AI deployment at scale.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI AG domain. Knowledge AG-K-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-002 (DG, cosine: 0.474), LS-K-008 (LS, cosine: 0.460).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.5/10 (raw 0.354). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, organizational]. NICE-only terms [cybersecurity, goals, objectives] vs WIDAI-only terms [accountability, ai, assignment, conditions, deployment, enable] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.882 (P=0.909, R=0.857). P exceeds R by 0.052 — the NICE element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (93.5%). "Knowledge of organizational cybersecurity goals and objectives" and "Knowledge of AI governance structures, including model risk management, AI lifec" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.478 > STS 0.354 (gap +0.124). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge, organizational] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.4%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, organizational]. NICE-only: [cybersecurity, goals, objectives]. WIDAI-only: [accountability, ai, assignment, conditions, deployment, enable].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.124 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
